evidence: CSRF token via authenticity_token meta tag

findings.title: Admin Panel Exposed on All API Subdomains
findings.details: All three API subdomains serve the same Acorns Administration login panel at /sign_in. Devise-based email/password auth. No rate limiting observed. Title: 'Acorns Admin'. Panel heading: 'Acorns Administration'.
findings.recommendation: Restrict admin panel access via IP allowlist or VPN. Add brute-force protection.

findings.title: Runtime/Request-ID Header Disclosure
findings.details: x-runtime and x-request-id headers exposed. x-runtime leaks processing time (timing attacks). x-request-id leaks UUID for log correlation.
findings.recommendation: Remove x-runtime and x-request-id from production responses.

findings.title: CORS Vary:Origin Active - No Wildcard Reflection
findings.details: Vary: Origin header present on all endpoints when Origin header sent. evil.com was NOT reflected in Access-Control-Allow-Origin (good). Dynamic CORS handling confirmed - test further with authenticated sessions and acorns.io origins.
findings.recommendation: Test with authenticated sessions. Verify no wildcard or overly permissive CORS with credentials.

findings.title: Health Endpoint Exposed
findings.details: /health returns 200 with server timestamp. Reveals timezone (PDT -0700). All three subdomains share identical backend.

findings.title: Sidekiq Admin Redirects to admin.acorns.com
findings.details: /sidekiq returns 302 to admin.acorns.com/sign_in. Confirms Sidekiq (Redis) as background job processor.

findings.title: Session Cookie SameSite=None
findings.details: _session_id cookie uses Secure + HttpOnly + SameSite=None. SameSite=None allows cross-site cookie sending which could enable CSRF if CORS is misconfigured.

findings.title: CSP Allows unsafe-inline and unsafe-eval
findings.details: Content-Security-Policy allows 'unsafe-inline' and 'unsafe-eval' in script-src, weakening XSS protection. Also loads from Google Analytics and Google APIs.

findings.title: Expired Origin-Trial Token Leaks acorns.io Domain
findings.details: origin-trial meta tag contains base64 payload revealing acorns.io:443 as primary domain with Chrome feature DisableDifferentOriginSubframeDialogSuppression. Expired (2021-12-15).

auth_bypass.conclusion: No API endpoints accessible without auth. /api/v1/* paths return 404. API likely requires token-based auth with different route structure or different subdomain.

cors.details: Vary: Origin present but evil.com not reflected in ACAO header.
cors.conclusion: CORS processing active but not exploitable without auth. Needs authenticated testing.

mass_assignment.details: POST /api/v1/users with {user:{email,role,admin}} returns 404. Endpoint not found.
mass_assignment.conclusion: Cannot test mass assignment without discovering valid API endpoints.

idor.conclusion: Standard REST IDOR not testable. API likely uses UUIDs or different routing.

next_steps: Discover actual mobile/app API endpoint structure (likely different from /api/v1/*)
Test CORS with authenticated session and acorns.io origin
Attempt login brute-force on admin panel (with rate-limit testing)
Discover API documentation or mobile app SDK to find real endpoints
Test for JWT/token-based auth bypass on mobile API
Test /sign_in for default credentials or password reset abuse
